title: Memory, Storage, RAID, and Removable Media · Fault-Isolation Clinic
summary: A realistic memory, storage, raid, and removable media ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in memory and storage integrity decision record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare RAM generation, form factor, capacity, channels, speed, timing, voltage, ECC, registered status, compatibility, and population rules.
Distinguish capacity pressure, application faults, thermal instability, compatibility, contact, and hardware errors using repeatable evidence.
Compare magnetic, flash, optical, removable, SATA, SAS, NVMe, form factor, interface, endurance, latency, throughput, and workload fit.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Storage Health Custody
steps.instruction:
Order the Storage Health Custody CompTIA A+ support HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Storage Health Custody.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Storage Health Custody assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Storage Health Custody, keep Storage Health Custody connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Crashes, storage pressure, a degraded array, and a healthy-looking drive blur capacity, hardware, filesystem, redundancy, backup, and custody boundaries. The debug variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Preserve data custody while assessing capacity, SMART or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Storage Health Custody has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Storage Health Custody
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Storage Health Custody: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Crashes, storage pressure, a degraded array, and a healthy-looking drive blur capacity, hardware, filesystem, redundancy, backup, and custody boundaries. The debug variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Preserve data custody while assessing capacity, SMART or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Storage Health Custody.
steps.why: Retrieving Storage Health Custody through the concrete memory and storage integrity decision record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Storage Health Custody, keep Storage Health Custody connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve data custody while assessing capacity, SMART or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition.
